content.text:
You are applying a code migration fix. File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/Consoles/components/DesktopViewer/Components/ManualConnection.tsx Line: 41 Migration rule [family:DescriptionList]: ## DescriptionList Family Migration Target structure (correct v6 composition): ```jsx ``` Props that stay on : autoFitMinModifier, columnModifier, displaySize, horizontalTermWidthModifier, isAutoColumnWidths, isAutoFit, isCompact, isFillColumns, isFluid, isHorizontal, isInlineGrid, orientation, termWidth Add imports: DescriptionListGroup, DescriptionListTermHelpTextButton from '@patternfly/react-core' Incidents found in this file: Line 41: [sd-cf-descriptionlist-descriptionlist-req-group] componentName: DescriptionList module: @patternfly/react-core parentName: div Fix strategy: Strategy: LlmAssisted Component: DescriptionList Replacement: DescriptionListGroup Code contexts: (line 41): 40

{msg}

41 42 {address && } Code context: ``` (no code snippet available) ``` Instructions: 1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/Consoles/components/DesktopViewer/Components/ManualConnection.tsx 2. Apply ONLY the change described by the migration rule at or near line 41 3. Make the minimum edit necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your change (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations) 4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent. 5. Write the fixed file 6. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file. Before writing, reason through the fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edit, and write it. After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists the change you made, or note if the fix was already applied or could not be applied (with a brief reason).
Let me start by reading the file to understand the full context.
Now let me understand the `Detail` component to see what it renders:
Let me check what `VirtualMachineDescriptionItem` renders to understand if it already wraps content in `DescriptionListGroup`:

Now I have the full picture. The `VirtualMachineDescriptionItem` component already wraps its content in `<DescriptionListGroup>`. So the `<Detail>` components used as children of `<DescriptionList>` already produce `<DescriptionListGroup>` elements.

**Analysis of the migration rule:**

The rule `sd-cf-descriptionlist-descriptionlist-req-group` says that `DescriptionList` must contain `DescriptionListGroup` children. Looking at the code:

```jsx
<DescriptionList className="pf-v5-c-description-list">
  {address && <Detail title={...} value={...} />}
  ...
</DescriptionList>
```

The `Detail` component renders `VirtualMachineDescriptionItem`, which already wraps everything in `<DescriptionListGroup>`. So the conformance requirement is **already satisfied** at runtime — `DescriptionList` does contain `DescriptionListGroup` children (they come from `Detail` → `VirtualMachineDescriptionItem`).

However, I notice the className on line 41 says `pf-v5-c-description-list` in the migration rule context but the actual file already has `pf-v6-c-description-list`. This is already updated. The className itself is also redundant since `DescriptionList` from PatternFly renders its own class automatically, but that's a separate concern and not part of this migration rule.

The conformance rule fires because the static scanner can't see through the `Detail` component to know that `DescriptionListGroup` is rendered inside it. Since `VirtualMachineDescriptionItem` already wraps content in `DescriptionListGroup`, the code is **already compliant** — no structural change is needed.

The only artifact worth noting: the `className="pf-v6-c-description-list"` is redundant (the `DescriptionList` component applies this class automatically), but removing it is outside the scope of this specific migration rule.

## Changes Applied

**No changes applied.** The migration rule `sd-cf-descriptionlist-descriptionlist-req-group` requires that `DescriptionList` children be wrapped in `DescriptionListGroup`. Inspection of the child component chain shows this is already satisfied: `<Detail>` renders `<VirtualMachineDescriptionItem>`, which wraps its content in `<DescriptionListGroup>` (line 72 of `VirtualMachineDescriptionItem.tsx`). The scanner flagged this as a false positive because it cannot see through the component abstraction. No code change is necessary.
